name: RNode (Reticulum LoRa)
label: RNode — Reticulum LoRa radio interface
description: markqvist/RNode_Firmware (GPL-3.0) — turns an ESP32/S3 LoRa board (T-Beam, T3S3, Heltec V3, LoRa32, T-Deck, XIAO-S3) into a Reticulum (RNS) radio interface. LoRa comms transport; no attack/jammer/deauth. It is a user-tunable TX radio, so operate only on bands you are licensed/authorized for. Cyber Controller flashes the vendor firmware and adds no capability of its own.
note: esptool multi-part flash from ONE per-board .zip (new per_board_zip resolver mode). Members verified from rnode_firmware_heltec32v3.zip @ tag 1.86: rnode_firmware_{board}.{bootloader,partitions,boot_app0,bin} + console_image.bin. Offsets: bootloader chip-dependent via _bootloader_offset (0x0 s3 / 0x1000 classic esp32), partitions 0x8000, boot_app0 0xe000, app 0x10000, console_image (SPIFFS, optional) 0x210000. nRF boards (rak4631/heltec_t114/techo) EXCLUDED — Nordic, not esptool. danger '' — legitimate LoRa transport (not a jammer/deauth); the tunable-TX 'licensed use only' caveat lives in the description + the universal legal disclaimer (same posture as meshtastic/meshcore/nautilus). FUNCTIONAL caveat: a flashed RNode boots but is NOT rnodeconf-provisioned/EEPROM-signed (device-signing is out of scope for CC) — full Reticulum operation needs `rnodeconf`. Offsets/members kept verify: (release.json ships a per-ZIP SHA, not per-member) until a real T3S3/T-Beam flash confirms boot (Phase-F HW gate).
